description: Local parameter shadowing in DoctorShiba.allowance: the `owner` parameter shadows the inherited `Ownable.owner()` function. The allowance getter itself is read-only, but the naming collision can confuse review of ERC20 holder approvals versus the privileged owner account.

locations.label: shadowing parameter in allowance
shadowed Ownable.owner function

description: Local parameter shadowing in DoctorShiba._approve: the internal `_approve` parameter named `owner` shadows `Ownable.owner()`. This finding is distinct from the approve race condition: Slither flags naming ambiguity, while the Forge PoC demonstrates the ERC20 allowance replacement/double-spend behavior.

locations.label: shadowing parameter in _approve
shadowed Ownable.owner function

description: Missing zero-address validation for `recipient` in DoctorShiba.transferFrom before assigning `newun`. When `newun` is unset and `sender` is nonzero, the first transferFrom recipient is stored directly as `newun`; Slither flags that this state assignment does not reject `address(0)`.

locations.label: recipient assigned to newun in transferFrom

description: Missing zero-address validation in DoctorShiba.transfernewun: the owner can set `newun` to any supplied address, including `address(0)`. Since `transferFrom` uses `newun` in recipient restrictions, an unchecked update can alter that restriction state unexpectedly.

locations.label: newun later used in transferFrom restriction

description: Timestamp dependency in Ownable.unlock: the function relies on `now > _lockTime` to decide when ownership can be restored to `_previousOwner`. This is a low-impact timestamp-comparison finding in Slither; it is separate from the approve race PoC, but relevant to ownership-locking behavior in the same contract.

locations.label: timestamp comparison controlling unlock
lock function sets _lockTime from now
